title: The Briar Lane incident
briefing: Mara Ellison, 34, hospice nurse. Works overnight shifts. She moved into a house on Briar Lane about two months ago. Over the last two weeks she's been experiencing things she can't explain — faint sounds above the garage at night, small objects not where she left them, and twice when coming home after midnight she saw what looked like a figure standing in the upper window. She called the police. They walked through, found nothing, told her it was probably the house settling. She had a security system installed three weeks ago by a local company — the cameras have picked up nothing definitive. She doesn't know what's going on, but she knows something isn't right. She's hired you to find out what it is.
solution: Mara Ellison's house was not haunted. Miles Kerr, the alarm installer from Sentinel Ridge Security, had developed an unhealthy fixation on the house during the installation and began secretly returning at night. He used a loose soffit gap above the garage, climbed up the exterior wall using the brick joints and gutter line, and squeezed into the eaves cavity over the bonus room. From there he could lie silently for hours and later descend through the closet attic access after Mara fell asleep or left for work. He avoided detection by wrapping himself in a mylar emergency blanket to distort thermal readings and by moving only when the HVAC cycled or adjacent lights shifted. Colin Wren, the next-door neighbor, saw suspicious movement near the roofline on multiple nights but concealed it because Mara had already accused him once of watching her house too closely, which made him look guilty without being the intruder. The case is solved by the exterior shoe scuffs, attic insulation path, service logs proving Miles returned alone after the install, the hidden supplies in the eaves, Colin's window photos, and the final thermal sweep showing a man-sized heat signature above the garage.

suspects.name: Dylan Rook
suspects.alibi: Bartending at Low Lantern from 7:00 PM to 1:30 AM on the night of the window sighting
suspects.motive: Resentment over the breakup and anger that Mara changed the locks after moving
what_they_know: Mara told him she kept hearing soft movement above the garage late at night
Mara said she had started leaving lights on upstairs because the house felt wrong
what_they_lie_about: He claims he never drove past the house after the breakup, but a plate reader places him on Briar Lane two nights earlier
He downplays how upset he was when Mara refused to let him inside the week before

suspects.name: Grant Vale
suspects.alibi: Repairing a rental property on Mason Street until after 10:00 PM
suspects.motive: Mara refused to hire him for additional work after accusing him of leaving the garage trim unfinished
what_they_know: The soffit above the garage had a narrow gap where fascia work had never been fully sealed
There was a closet attic hatch in the upstairs guest room
what_they_lie_about: He says he never mentioned the soffit gap to anyone, though he noted it in a text
He omits that he returned once to the property to look at the gutter line from the driveway

suspects.name: Owen Pike
suspects.alibi: Completing a late rodent exclusion job in North Creek from 8:00 PM to 11:45 PM
suspects.motive: Mara had complained that he charged her without solving the attic-noise issue
what_they_know: He found no rodent nesting despite signs of intermittent movement in the attic insulation
He noticed the attic access hatch looked recently handled, not dusty like the surrounding trim
what_they_lie_about: He says he never went beyond the garage ladder area, though his service notes mention the upstairs hatch
He denies telling Mara that a person could theoretically fit through the soffit line

suspects.name: Colin Wren
suspects.alibi: At his brother's house watching a game from 8:15 PM to 11:40 PM on the night of the window sighting
suspects.motive: Mara had accused him of watching her house too closely after he twice commented on lights turning on upstairs when she was not home
what_they_know: He saw movement near the garage roofline on two separate nights but did not report it because he thought Mara would accuse him again
He noticed the upstairs guest-room light come on briefly when Mara was still at work
He heard a metallic scrape near the gutter line around midnight the week before investigators were hired
what_they_lie_about: He says he never looked toward Mara's house at night, but he has phone photos of the upper window taken from his driveway
He downplays how often he monitored the property after suspecting someone was getting onto the roof

suspects.name: Miles Kerr
suspects.relationship_to_victim: Alarm installer from Sentinel Ridge Security
suspects.alibi: At home after finishing an installation across town by 8:30 PM
suspects.motive: He developed an obsessive attachment to the house and its routines and wanted to inhabit it secretly without being seen, describing himself later as wanting to be 'part of the house'
what_they_know: He knew the layout of the upstairs hall, the guest-room closet attic access, and the blind angles of the hallway camera
He returned alone two days after installation on the pretext of calibrating the panel
He hid in the eaves cavity above the garage and used a mylar blanket to distort thermal readings
what_they_lie_about: He says he never came back to the property after the installation was complete
He says the house had no practical entry point outside the doors and windows
He denies noticing the soffit gap or the closet hatch during the installation walk-through

timeline.event: Sentinel Ridge Security installs Mara's alarm system and interior cameras
Miles Kerr returns alone for a brief panel calibration visit
Mara hears a slow creak and faint movement above the garage after returning from work
Mara photographs what appears to be a person standing in the upper window over the garage
Patrol officers conduct a brief interior walk-through and find no intruder
Investigators are hired and conduct the first site interview with Mara
Hallway camera captures a brightness shift and a partial dark edge in the guest-room doorway, but no clear person
Exterior inspection reveals shoe scuffs on the brick and a bent gutter bracket beneath the garage eave
Colin Wren admits he photographed the upstairs window on an earlier night after seeing a silhouette while Mara was still at work
Attic probe shows compressed insulation leading from the soffit gap toward the guest-room closet hatch
A thermal sweep after HVAC shutdown reveals a man-sized heat signature lying motionless above the garage
Officers remove Miles Kerr from the eaves space with a mylar blanket, flashlight, and bottled water
Miles states in custody that he never meant to hurt Mara and only wanted to remain inside the house quietly as 'part of it'

evidence.description: Client phone image shows a narrow human silhouette in the upper window above the garage at 1:56 AM
evidence.points_to: A real person was inside the house rather than a reflection or exterior passerby

evidence.description: Fresh shoe scuffs on the garage wall brick, chalky hand smears, a bent gutter bracket beneath the eave, and disturbed debris along the roofline — found only on close exterior examination
evidence.points_to: Someone repeatedly climbed the exterior wall to reach the upper eaves

evidence.description: Hallway camera captures only a shifting shadow and a partial dark edge near the guest-room doorway — no full body in frame, nothing conclusive
evidence.points_to: A person was moving just outside normal camera coverage

evidence.description: Thermal sweep after HVAC shutdown reveals a human-sized hotspot lying motionless in the eaves cavity above the garage
evidence.points_to: An intruder was physically occupying the house structure

evidence.description: Compressed insulation and joist impressions form a clear path from the soffit gap toward the upstairs closet hatch, with a hollow depression where a person could lie flat
evidence.points_to: The intruder used the eaves cavity as a concealed route into the house interior

evidence.description: A mylar emergency blanket, water bottles, protein bar wrappers, and a small flashlight are found hidden in the eaves cavity above the garage
evidence.points_to: The intruder intended repeated covert occupancy rather than a single break-in

evidence.description: Sentinel Ridge records show Miles Kerr returned alone for an unsupervised follow-up visit two days after the initial install — logged as 'panel calibration,' duration 18 minutes, no co-technician present
evidence.points_to: Miles Kerr had legitimate interior access after learning the home's layout, and returned alone

evidence.description: Cross-referencing installer notes and camera placement diagrams against Mara's reported activity nights shows Miles mapped the upstairs blind angles and closet access area during the security install — and the nights she reported sounds match his service visit windows
evidence.points_to: Miles Kerr had advance knowledge of every camera blind spot and the sole interior attic access point

evidence.description: Phone photos from Colin Wren's driveway — taken on a night Mara was still at work — show a dim human silhouette in the upper window above the garage
evidence.points_to: Someone was inside the house before Mara returned home, ruling out Mara herself as the source of the sighting

evidence.description: Fibers and a biological trace sample collected from the closet hatch and the mylar emergency blanket surface
evidence.analysis: Touch DNA profile from the biological sample matches Miles Kerr; fiber composition is consistent with his documented work jacket

evidence.description: Miles states he was living in the eaves space because he liked being inside the house quietly and felt he belonged there
evidence.points_to: Miles Kerr — motive confirmed as obsessive fixation, not financial or predatory

evidence.description: Initial interior walkthrough of 14 Briar Lane. Nothing appears disturbed or out of place. House layout noted: living area and kitchen on ground floor, garage to the side, bonus room above the garage accessed via upstairs hall. Guest-room closet has a standard ceiling-mounted attic access panel — unremarkable, like most houses of this age.
evidence.points_to: Establishes house layout and attic hatch location for reference

evidence.description: Close examination of the garage roofline reveals a narrow gap in the soffit where fascia work was never fully sealed — wide enough for a person to squeeze through with effort. The gap is not visible from ground level without specifically looking for it.
evidence.points_to: A concealed exterior entry point into the eaves cavity above the garage

evidence.description: During a street canvass, Colin Wren mentions that he has occasionally noticed an unfamiliar vehicle — a dark-colored compact van — parked on Briar Lane for an hour or two some evenings over the past three weeks. He doesn't recall plates. He seems uncomfortable and keeps the conversation brief.
evidence.points_to: An unexplained recurring vehicle presence near the property during the activity window

evidence.description: Cross-referencing Miles Kerr's service visit log against the dates Mara reported hearing sounds reveals a pattern: the first incident occurred two nights after his solo follow-up visit on October 5th, and subsequent activity clusters around evenings when his dispatch records show no assigned jobs — leaving his whereabouts unaccounted for.
evidence.points_to: Miles Kerr's schedule overlaps with every documented incident window

documents.content: Mara Ellison reports repeated sounds above the garage, slight object displacement in the upstairs guest room, and two sightings of a figure in the upper window after returning from hospice shifts.
Officers responded to a possible intruder call at 2:24 AM. Interior sweep located no suspect. No broken doors or windows were observed. Complainant advised to review camera footage.
Sentinel Ridge Security installation contract lists Miles Kerr as lead installer with full interior walk-through, camera placement responsibility, and panel programming access.
Service ticket shows Miles Kerr returned alone on October 5 for 'panel calibration / sensor check.' Duration recorded at 18 minutes with no co-technician present.
Overnight footage shows multiple light shifts and partial shadow movement near the guest-room threshold without a full identifiable subject entering the hallway view.

Colin Wren initially denies seeing anything unusual at Mara Ellison's house. He later admits he observed movement near the garage roofline on multiple nights and photographed the upstairs window after seeing a silhouette.
Thermal imaging performed after HVAC deactivation detected a concentrated human-sized heat signature in the eaves cavity above the garage, inconsistent with ducting, wiring, or animal presence.
Miles Kerr states he never intended to hurt Mara Ellison and says he felt calm lying inside the house structure, describing himself as wanting to be 'part of the house' rather than a visitor.

dossier.header: CASE FILE — The Eaves Above Briar Lane

narrative.opening_scene: Mara Ellison gets home at 1:56 in the morning, still in scrubs, still carrying her badge and lunch bag. Before she can unlock the front door, she glances up and sees a man-shaped figure standing in the dark upper window above the garage. By the time she backs into the driveway and looks again, the window is empty. Police find nothing. The cameras show shadows. Now it is your case.

mysterious_message.message_content: There is someone in my upstairs window again. Police already came once and found nothing. I think something is living in this house.

threads.subject: Question about upstairs camera coverage
threads.body: I keep getting strange movement upstairs but the hallway clip never shows a full person. Can someone confirm whether there are any blind spots near the guest-room closet or bonus-room window?

threads.subject: RE: Question about upstairs camera coverage
threads.body: Camera layout is standard and should cover normal traffic. If you want I can stop by sometime next week and take another look.

messages.body: I just saw someone in the upstairs window over the garage.
Are you serious? Call the police right now.
They came last time and found nothing. I am not imagining this.
There are sounds above the garage almost every night after I get home.
[DRAFT — UNSENT] If something is really up there then it knows when I come home.
